title: triticum sativum subsp. spelta
intro: Triticum sativum subsp. spelta, also known as spelt, is a subspecies of wheat that has been cultivated for thousands of years. It is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high levels of selenium, zinc, and iron. Consuming spelt has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Spelt can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, flour, and supplements, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions like arthritis and digestive issues.
